sections.heading: When to use
sections.text: Use an AHO-based virtual oscillator controller for three-phase inverter grid-forming operation when sinusoidal self-synchronizing behavior is desired and the requirement explicitly prefers oscillator-based regulation over VSG emulation.

sections.heading: Current-loop coordination
sections.text: Keep an inner current-limiting or current-regulating layer active beneath the oscillator so load-step and startup stress do not rely on oscillator dynamics alone. The oscillator should create the voltage waveform, while the current layer enforces protection.

sections.heading: Tradeoff
sections.text: AHO-based VOC can provide clean sinusoidal references and autonomous synchronization, but amplitude restoration, dispatchability, and current-limit interaction must be tuned carefully in weak-grid operation.
